chapter_title.en: Chapter on Endowments, Charity, and Gifts

text: 5594 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَبَّاسُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلصَّحَارِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ اِشْتَرَى دَاراً فَبَقِيَتْ عَرْصَةٌ فَبَنَاهَا بَيْتَ غَلَّةٍ أَ يُوقِفُهُ عَلَى اَلْمَسْجِدِ قَالَ "إِنَّ اَلْمَجُوسَ أَوْقَفُوا عَلَى بَيْتِ اَلنَّارِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5594 - Al-Abbas ibn Amir narrated from Abu Al-Sahari, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who bought a house, and there remained an empty plot of land. He built upon it a house to generate income. Should he endow it for the mosque?
Imam (as) replied: "Indeed, the Magians endowed property for their fire temples."
